title: Cult Adjacent Vacation
tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.
omen: vitamins that taste faintly of sedation and drywall

starter_premise.title: Cult Adjacent Vacation
starter_premise.premise: She goes on a retreat, the retreat is a cult, and escaping takes the whole movie.
starter_premise.signature_tell: The county telegraphs the problem once and nobody listens until act three.

props: throat lozenges with a suspicious aftertaste

cast.name: Gavin Winterson
traits: says she is fine in a voice that needs legal counsel
keeps a color-coded calendar and a cracked heart
secret_capacity: saw one text too many and said nothing for three weeks
has already priced out a life without him
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: dock office with a desk nobody fully claims
cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

cast.name: Rachel Hawthorne
traits: knows routines he should not know
secret_capacity: thinks apology gifts erase documentary evidence
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events
cast.space_description: church basement described as seasonal materials

cast.name: Mara Pruitt
traits: keeps throat lozenges in a bag that feels doctrinally wrong
asks for passwords with fake embarrassment
secret_capacity: knows how to mimic the protagonist's voicemail tone
cast.regional_responsibility: organizes a fundraiser with tense undertones

secluded_space_access.space_type: side room with a space heater

cast.space_description: side room with a space heater and old family records

cast.name: Brooke Pike
traits: seems one kitchen confession away from headline syntax
says he would not stop in a way nobody enjoys hearing twice
smiles like a restraining order is considering her
secret_capacity: bought bleach for innocent reasons that no longer sound innocent
rehearsed a self-defense story before anything was proven
cast.regional_responsibility: has keys to a church annex
cast.space_description: dock office with a desk nobody fully claims
cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

cast.name: Daniel Hawthorne
traits: forgot a recital once and never recovered
secret_capacity: still has access to a property he does not own
ignored a warning text on the worst possible night
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: annex room described as old family things
cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

secret_capacity: accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events

traits: notices timeline errors before adults do

cast.name: Cole Sutton
traits: knows the old case too well

beat_sheet.text: From scene one the category runs on dread: She goes on a retreat, the retreat is a cult, and escaping takes the whole movie. The audience is meant to feel wrong before Gavin Winterson has language for why, and the house in Blackthorn Lake feels curated for future testimony. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Rachel Hawthorne hesitates before explaining church basement described as seasonal materials. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Domestic normalcy is established only so it can be contaminated by Rachel Hawthorne, the children, the hallway, or one too-helpful stranger with keys. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

A dim kitchen or nursery scene introduces the boat key and one unmistakable omen for us but not for her. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Brooke Pike says something almost truthful and then retreats, leaving Gavin Winterson alone with the feeling of being watched. Signature tell: The county telegraphs the problem once and nobody listens until act three. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

Act one ends with the category proving that comfort itself is now suspect. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.31.

Domestic uncanny pressure spikes as ordinary rituals become contaminated: medication, childcare, bedding, internet searches, and locked doors all start feeling weaponized. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. The police, partner, or therapist do not call her insane outright, but they help reality become blurrier instead of clearer. The ally either starts to believe too late or gets close enough to become a target, because dread punishes proximity. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. By the end of act two, the threat is either confirmed or still technically deniable, but never emotionally ambiguous anymore. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. The house, retreat, clinic, or family system becomes openly hostile and escape starts to look like the only sane form of interpretation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Gavin Winterson finds the hidden room, second file, fake history, or prior victim trace that proves the dread had architecture all along. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.10. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

A confrontation involving vindictive music box turns survival into an act of improvised authorship rather than institutional rescue. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Even when the villain falls, the category leaves residue: the children, the paperwork, or the body memory of the place remain compromised. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Rachel Hawthorne hesitates before explaining church basement described as seasonal materials. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

The ending may close the plot, but it should never fully close the feeling. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Rachel Hawthorne is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Gavin Winterson says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Winter Hollow.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Rachel?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Derek. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Gavin. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The boat key is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Brooke."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "Cult Adjacent Vacation understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "Cult Adjacent Vacation" follows Gavin Winterson, a high-output betrayed wife whose life seemed manageable at first, even after emotionally charged casserole exchange opens a new room inside it. Rachel Hawthorne seemed helpful. The house seemed calm. Even vitamins that taste faintly of sedation and drywall could seemed incidental if anyone were still interested in the difference between comfort and staging. The governing injury is simple: She goes on a retreat, the retreat is a cult, and escaping takes the whole movie. The signature tell is always this: The county telegraphs the problem once and nobody listens until act three. Everything seemed fine until it very plainly is not, and the narrator keeps talking softly enough to make that failure feel deliberate. Love lives here. So does arson.
